hypothesis: If a 3D electron-density encoder is contrastively aligned with a SMILES encoder without explicit SE(3) equivariance or rotation augmentation, then it will learn pose-invariant molecular embeddings that outperform unimodal and SE(3)-equivariant baselines on unseen-rotation retrieval, functional-group recall, structure–property clustering, and linear-probe property prediction because the multimodal objective forces the 3D encoder to discard orientation and capture rotation-invariant chemical features shared with SMILES.
